info.title: EXPORTCOMMENTS.COM - API Documentation (BETA)
info.description: ### Introduction Welcome to the [ExportComments.com](https://exportcomments.com) API documentation. EXPORT COMMENTS API enables you to programatically manage and run your exports and retrieve extracted data. You can find sample code in a variety of languages. By default curl is selected so that you can try out the commands in your terminal. You may also be interested by our [Github account](https://github.com/exportcomments), a place where you can find api clients ready to use. ### Authentication Each request must include your API Key for authentication. You can find your API Key on your account page ### Requests `POST` requests must have a form-encoded body and the `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded; charset=utf-8` header. `GET` requests must be url-encoded All requests must be made over HTTPS. Any HTTP requests are responded to with a HTTP 302 to the equivalent HTTPS address. If you are using the curl examples, make sure that any data you replace is properly shell-escaped. ExportComments.com limits API usage to 5 requests per second, with any requests thereafter being queued up to a maximum of 5 requests. Requests thereafter will return a 429 status code. ### Responses Unless explicitly mentioned, JSON will be returned in all responses. ### Errors ExportComments returns standard HTTP errors when possible. # Objects There is one primary type of object that the ExportComments API operates with. ### Methods This section describes all of the HTTP endpoints that can be used to manipulate exports. The typical way to use the ExportComments API is the following: - Start an export This is done using the [`POST /api/v3/job`](#tag/job/post/api/v3/job) method below - Wait for the export to finish You can use one of: 1. [Webhooks](#tag/webhook/get/api/v1/webhooks) (recommended) 2. Polling the [`GET /api/v3/job/{guid}`](#tag/job/get/api/v3/job/{guid}) method below. Note there are rate limits on this method. - Get the data from the export Once the export `status` is `done`, you can download the data from either the ExportComments website or API.

get.summary: Retrieves the collection of API Tokens resources.
get.description: Retrieves the collection of API Tokens resources.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of items per page

expiresAt.description: The expiration date of the token

400.description: Invalid request or token generation failed

post.description: This endpoint generates a new API token for the authenticated user. The token will expire after one year.

get.description: Simple ping endpoint that returns pong to verify API is working

id.description: The ID of the created job.

guid.description: The globally unique identifier (GUID) of the job.

status.description: The status of the job (e.g., queueing, done).

url.description: The URL associated with the job.

options.description: The options applied to the job.

400.description: Invalid request or job creation failed

seconds_to_wait.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying.

post.description: This endpoint allows creating a new export process job for a user. It validates the input, applies rate limits, and queues the job for processing.

requestBody.description: Details of the export process job to be created.

url.description: The URL associated with the export process.

options.description: Additional options for the export process.

vpn.description: VPN settings for the export process. See VPN documentation for details.

pool.description: Proxy pool UUID to use for the export.

advanced.description: Enable advanced search options (applicable to Google Feature).

facebookAds.description: Whether to export Facebook Ads data only.

followers.description: Whether to export only followers in the export.

following.description: Whether to export only following data in the export.

id.description: Variable used for speeding up the export process.

likes.description: Whether to include likes in the export.

limit.description: The maximum number of items to export.

live.description: Whether to export live data only in the export.

maxTimestamp.description: The maximum timestamp for filtering data.

minTimestamp.description: The minimum timestamp for filtering data.

replies.description: Whether to include replies in the export.

shares.description: Whether to include shares in the export.

tweets.description: The type of Twitter export (e.g., tweets, followers, following).
